theme: Toll-free city removes roads to cut jams
title: Roadwork: Cutting Streets to Un-jam a Selfish City
strategies: trivial: keep only the free-flow shortest path and push all demand down it (ignores congestion; reproduces the single-path baseline)
greedy: keep the whole network and let the Wardrop equilibrium sort it out (walks into every Braess trap)
strong: equilibrium-aware edge deletion -- prune any edge whose removal strictly lowers the computed equilibrium travel time, exploiting non-monotonicity of equilibrium latency in the edge set
beyond-strong (open): combinatorial / global search over kept edge subsets, since single-edge local pruning is not optimal
